mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during documented rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, weak labor demand signals deteriorating consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with p-values above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal income and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and investment demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a statistically significant positive return differential with t-statistics below 1.5 compared to baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation above 215 triggers expectations of monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are non-negative in greater than 65% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 215, or the signal reverses direction during low-rate environments.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production indices above 90 reflect robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of periods where INDPRO exceeds 90, or the correlation between INDPRO levels and forward returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm genuine labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative or flat forward return distribution, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-values above 0.05 in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly expands aggregate corporate revenue pools, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across rolling 5-year windows, or out-of-sample R-squared remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with stagnant industrial production signals stagflationary pressures that erode real corporate profits and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 218 and INDPRO growth is flat, or the joint condition fails to predict return direction with greater than 55% accuracy.
